Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze a specific event log for an Insurance Claims Processing process I need to Identify cases with long durations performance issues Analyze attributes Resource Region Complexity to find root causes Propose explanations and mitigation strategies

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Data Extraction and Processing Mental or Scratchpad I need to calculate the duration Lead Time for each Case ID Start Time First event timestamp per case End Time Last event Close Claim timestamp per case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 Start 2024 04 01 09 00 End 2024 04 01 10 30 Duration 1 hour 30 minutes 90 mins Very fast

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 Start 2024 04 01 09 05 End 2024 04 02 11 00 Duration 25 hours 55 mins Includes an overnight gap likely due to business hours or waiting time Request Additional Documents happens on day 1 afternoon Approve is next morning This seems reasonable but longer than 2001 2004

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 Start 2024 04 01 09 10 End 2024 04 03 09 30 Duration 48 hours 20 mins 2 plus days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 Start 2024 04 01 09 20 End 2024 04 01 10 45 Duration 1 hour 25 minutes Very fast

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 Start 2024 04 01 09 25 End 2024 04 04 14 30 Duration 77 hours 3 days plus 5 hours This is the longest by far

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Step 1 Identify Performance Issues Long Cases Fast Cases 2001 2004 Duration less than 2 hours Medium Case 2002 Duration about 26 hours Slow Problematic Cases 2003 and 2005 These are the outliers in terms of total duration

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Complexity Fast 2001 Low Fast 2004 Low Medium 2002 Medium Slow 2003 High Slow 2005 High Correlation Strong correlation between High complexity and long duration This is a primary suspect

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region Fast 2001 A Fast 2004 B Medium 2002 B Slow 2003 A Slow 2005 B Analysis Region A has one fast and one slow Region B has one fast one medium one slow No obvious regional bias causing slowness in isolation both regions have high complexity cases that are slow So Region is not the sole driver

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Adjuster Mike handled 2001 Fast Low and 2003 Slow High Adjuster Lisa handled 2004 Fast Low 2002 Medium Medium 2005 Slow High Observation Both adjusters handle fast and slow cases It depends heavily on the case they get Complexity

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Crucial Detail Look at the Request Additional Documents loop 2003 High two requests 11 00 17 00 gap of 6 hours same day 2005 High three requests 11 30 Day 1 17 00 Day 2 15 00 Day 3 huge gaps here The process stalled multiple times 2002 Medium one request 14 00 approved next morning

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Manager Ann handled 2001 Fast 2004 Fast 2002 Medium All relatively quick approvals once they got the case Manager Bill handled 2003 Slow and 2005 Slow Observation Manager Bill only handles High complexity cases in this log Is Manager Bill slow Or does he only get the hard cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 2003 Approve happened 16 00 Day 2 Last doc request was 17 00 Day 1 that is a 23 hour wait for approval after the last document 2005 Approve happened 10 00 Day 4 Last doc request was 15 00 Day 3 that is a 19 hour wait Comparison Manager Ann approved 2002 at 10 00 Day 2 request was 14 00 Day 1 to 20 hour wait The approval lag looks similar overnight

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Finance Alan 2001 Fast 2003 Slow Finance Carl 2004 Fast 2002 Medium 2005 Slow Observation Follows the case speed

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deep Dive into the Bottleneck The defining characteristic of 2003 and 2005 is not just that they are High Complexity It is the number of iterations of Request Additional Documents Case 2003 had 2 requests Total time about 2 days Case 2005 had 3 requests Total time about 3 days Case 2002 Medium had 1 request Total time about 1 day Case 2001 2004 Low had 0 requests Total time less than 2 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesizing Root Causes 1 Complexity Level The strongest driver High complexity leads to rework loops 2 The Rework Loop Process inefficiency Specifically the back and forth for documents 3 Resource Availability Handling Time

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2005 Eval 9 50 to Request 1 11 30 okay Request 1 11 30 to Request 2 next day 17 00 wait Customer took a day Or Adjuster Lisa did not check until end of next day Request 2 17 00 to Request 3 Day 3 15 00 another long gap This suggests either slow customer response external factor adjuster backlog delays in checking incoming docs internal factor resource issue manager availability

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Part 2 Root Cause Analysis Factor 1 Claim Complexity show the direct correlation High equals Long Factor 2 Rework Loops quantify the number of Request Additional Docs Explain how each iteration adds significant time often days Factor 3 Resource and Regional nuances Note that Manager Bill is associated with all slow cases but only gets High complexity Note that Region B has the absolute worst case 2005 Note the specific delays in the Evaluate Request phase handled by Adjuster Mike and Adjuster Lisa

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Part 3 Explanations and Mitigation Strategies Complexity Better initial triage Clearer instructions for customers submitting high complexity claims to prevent missing docs Rework Why are docs missing Proactive checklist Resources Is Adjuster Lisa overloaded She handles 3 cases including the longest one Does Manager Bill have capacity issues

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refining the Calculations Self Correction Double Check Case 2001 09 00 to 10 30 1h 30m Case 2002 09 05 Apr 1 to 11 00 Apr 2 1 day 1h 55m Case 2003 09 10 Apr 1 to 09 30 Apr 3 2 days 0h 20m Case 2004 09 20 Apr 1 to 10 45 Apr 1 1h 25m Case 2005 09 25 Apr 1 to 14 30 Apr 4 3 days 5h 05m Conclusion remains valid 2003 and 2005 are the problems

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Final Polish of Narrative Make sure to explicitly link the attributes requested Resource Region Complexity to the findings Complexity The main differentiator Resource Adjusters handling high complexity seem stuck in loops Managers handling them might be bottlenecks or victims of the input delay Region Not the primary cause but Region B contains the worst performer Case 2005
